rationale: The citing paper’s core topic (psychological distance effects on left-vs-right digit use in separator-based numbers) is quite different from the cited paper’s focus (brand commitment and communication spillover), which weakens evidence of a direct substantive link. However, both notes explicitly involve perceived diagnosticity, and the citing paper uses diagnosticity as a key mechanism, so the citation could still be a conceptual/process source. Because the matched evidence is only a single DOI record with no local citation context, this is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only.
